entries.name: Roll Calculator — Boss Saves
entries.description: STR Save: Advantage 1d20+7. DEX Save: Advantage 1d20+9. CON Save: Advantage 1d20+10. INT Save: Advantage 1d20+8. WIS Save: Advantage 1d20+9. CHA Save: Advantage 1d20+15.

entries.name: Roll Calculator — Social / Casting
entries.description: Spell Save DC: 25. Spellcasting / Flesh Attack: Advantage 1d20+17. Proficient Charisma Skill: Advantage 1d20+15. Prepared Deception / Impersonation / Persuasion: 1d20+15; Silver Tongue d20 floor 10 applies. Player Improv Deception / Impersonation / Persuasion: 1d20+10; Expert Deceiver and Silver Tongue do not apply vs players.

entries.name: Advantage on All Rolls
entries.description: While Boss Presence is active, Capella rolls 2d20 and takes the higher result on attacks, saves, checks, and contested rolls unless a line says otherwise.

entries.name: Charisma Spellcasting
entries.description: Capella uses Charisma for spell save DCs, spell attacks, flesh magic, Polymorph effects, and distortion effects.

entries.name: Expert Deceiver
entries.description: Umbrella social mastery package. Grants prepared Deception / Impersonation / Persuasion a +10 expertise-style bonus. Does not apply against player characters.

entries.name: Silver Tongue
entries.description: College of Eloquence-style feature. For Deception and Persuasion, d20 rolls of 9 or lower count as 10. This is a d20 floor of 10. Does not apply against player character Insight checks.

entries.name: Arcane Mastery
entries.description: Adds +2 to spell save DCs and spell/flesh attack rolls.

entries.name: Unnatural Armor
entries.description: Base AC becomes 15 + Dexterity modifier while unarmored. With Boss DEX 18, this gives AC 19. Armored Flesh can raise this to AC 21.

entries.name: Aberrant Lich Bonus
entries.description: Adds +2 CHA and supports lich-style undead/Far Realm traits such as Soul Vessel, Far Realm Mutation, Unnatural Armor, Arcane Mastery, and Telepathy.

entries.name: Far Realm Mutation
entries.description: Explains psychic/cold/necrotic/acid resistance, poison/lightning/nonmagical weapon immunity, unstable biology, and body horror transformations.

entries.name: Soul Vessel / Far Realm Parasite
entries.description: When reduced to 0 HP, the current body collapses. Capella does not truly die unless the parasite/vessel anchor is found, contained, destroyed, or otherwise dealt with.

entries.name: Extra Attack
entries.description: When Capella takes the Attack action, she makes 2 attacks.

entries.name: Bonus Attack
entries.description: If Capella takes the Attack action on her turn, she may make 1 additional natural attack as a Bonus Action.

entries.name: Legendary Action
entries.description: Once per round, outside her turn, Capella may take 1 full Action. If used for Attack, this gives her 2 more attacks.

entries.name: Nonlethal Intent
entries.description: Capella usually avoids killing. Her attacks are meant to disable, maim, pin, terrify, transform, or humiliate rather than execute.

entries.name: Telepathy

rawImport.name: Telepathy
rawImport.desc:
Telepathy is a magical ability that allows a monster to communicate mentally with another creature within a specified range. The contacted creature doesn’t need to share a language with the monster to communicate in this way with it, but it must be able to understand at least one language. A creature without telepathy can receive and respond to telepathic messages but can’t initiate or terminate a telepathic conversation.

A telepathic monster doesn’t need to see a contacted creature and can end the telepathic contact at any time. The contact is broken as soon as the two creatures are no longer within range of each other or if the telepathic monster contacts a different creature within range. A telepathic monster can initiate or terminate a telepathic conversation without using an action, but while the monster is incapacitated, it can’t initiate telepathic contact, and any current contact is terminated.

A creature within the area of an *antimagic field* or in any other location where magic doesn’t function can’t send or receive telepathic messages.

entries.name: Eldritch Invocation: Beguiling Influence

rawImport.name: Eldritch Invocation: Beguiling Influence
desc: You gain proficiency in the Deception and Persuasion skills.

entries.description: You gain proficiency in the Deception and Persuasion skills.

entries.name: Lich

actions.name: Deathly Teleport
actions.desc: The lich teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see, and each creature within 10 feet of the space it left takes 11 (2d10) Necrotic damage.

actions.name: Disrupt Life
actions.desc: Constitution Saving Throw: DC 20, each creature that isn't an Undead in a 20-foot Emanation originating from the lich. Failure: 31 (9d6) Necrotic damage. Success: Half damage. Failure or Success: The lich can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

actions.name: Eldritch Burst
actions.desc: Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +12, reach 5 ft. or range 120 ft. 31 (4d12 + 5) Force damage.

actions.name: Frightening Gaze
actions.desc: The lich casts Fear, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting. The lich can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The lich makes three attacks, using Eldritch Burst or Paralyzing Touch in any combination.

actions.name: Paralyzing Touch
actions.desc: Melee Attack Roll: +12, reach 5 ft. 15 (3d6 + 5) Cold damage, and the target has the Paralyzed condition until the start of the lich's next turn.

actions.name: Spellcasting
actions.desc:
The lich casts one of the following spells, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 20):

- **At Will:** Detect Magic, Detect Thoughts, Dispel Magic, Fireball, Invisibility, Lightning Bolt, Mage Hand, Prestidigitation
- **2/Day Each:** Animate Dead, Dimension Door, Plane Shift
- **1/Day Each:** Chain Lightning, Finger of Death, Power Word Kill, Scrying

actions.name: Protective Magic
actions.desc: The lich casts Counterspell or Shield in response to the spell's trigger, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

traits.name: Legendary Resistance (4/Day, or 5/Day in Lair)
traits.desc: If the lich fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

traits.name: Spirit Jar
traits.desc: If destroyed, the lich reforms in 1d10 days if it has a spirit jar, reviving with all its Hit Points. The new body appears in an unoccupied space within the lich's lair.

entries.name: Charisma

rawImport.name: Charisma
rawImport.desc:
Charisma measures your ability to interact effectively with others. It includes such factors as confidence and eloquence, and it can represent a charming or commanding personality.

## Charisma Checks

A Charisma check might arise when you try to influence or entertain others, when you try to make an impression or tell a convincing lie, or when you are navigating a tricky social situation. The Deception, Intimidation, Performance, and Persuasion skills reflect aptitude in certain kinds of Charisma checks.

### Deception

Your Charisma (Deception) check determines whether you can convincingly hide the truth, either verbally or through your actions. This deception can encompass everything from misleading others through ambiguity to telling outright lies. Typical situations include trying to fast-talk a guard, con a merchant, earn money through gambling, pass yourself off in a disguise, dull someone's suspicions with false assurances, or maintain a straight face while telling a blatant lie.

### Intimidation  
When you attempt to influence someone through overt threats, hostile actions, and physical violence, the GM might ask you to make a Charisma (Intimidation) check. Examples include trying to pry information out of a prisoner, convincing street thugs to back down from a confrontation, or using the edge of a broken bottle to convince a sneering vizier to reconsider a decision.

### Performance  
Your Charisma (Performance) check determines how well you can delight an audience with music, dance, acting, storytelling, or some other form of entertainment.

### Persuasion  
When you attempt to influence someone or a group of people with tact, social graces, or good nature, the GM might ask you to make a Charisma (Persuasion) check. Typically, you use persuasion when acting in good faith, to foster friendships, make cordial requests, or exhibit proper etiquette. Examples of persuading others include convincing a chamberlain to let your party see the king, negotiating peace between warring tribes, or inspiring a crowd of townsfolk

### Other Charisma Checks

The GM might call for a Charisma check when you try to accomplish tasks like the following:

- Find the best person to talk to for news, rumors, and gossip
- Blend into a crowd to get the sense of key topics of conversation


## Spellcasting Ability

Bards, paladins, sorcerers, and warlocks use Charisma as their spellcasting ability, which helps determine the saving throw DCs of spells they cast.
